name: Jju
notes: STUB — DELIBERATELY LEFT UNFILLED. Jju (Du̱ryem Jju; Tyap: Jhyuo; Hausa: Kaje, Kache) is a Plateau (Tyapic, Atlantic-Congo) language of the Bajju people of Kaduna State, Nigeria (~600,000 speakers). WHY STILL A STUB: the phoneme inventory is documented (McKinney 1990, reproduced in the Wikipedia phonology tables — seven vowels /i e ɨ ə a o u/ plus long /aː oː/; a fortis-lenis (tense) contrast; a three-way plain / labialised [ʷ] / palatalised [ʲ] contrast on most consonants; allophonic palatalisation before front vowels and labialisation before rounded vowels), but NO normative letter-to-IPA table could be obtained. The one orthography statement referenced in the literature — Hyuwa, D. (1986), 'Kaje orthography', in Orthographies of Nigerian Languages, Manual 3/4, National Language Centre, Lagos — is not available online, and Omniglot's Jju alphabet chart is published only as an image with no machine-readable letter-to-sound values. What IS known about the writing system: Latin script; fortis consonants are written by DOUBLING the letter (hence the name ⟨Jju⟩); a consonant followed by ⟨w⟩ is labialised and one followed by ⟨y⟩ is palatalised; letters with a combining macron below occur (Ba̱jju, Ka̱jju, Du̱ryem). Filling the map would mean guessing the values of the doubled, macron-below and Cw/Cy sequences, so no graphemes are recorded: an honest stub beats a fabricated map. TO UNBLOCK: obtain Hyuwa (1986) or a current Bajju literacy / Bible-translation orthography guide. Jju is a Tyapic lect and the Tyap Literacy Committee chart (see spec kcg) is a plausible but UNVERIFIED starting point — it must not be copied over without a Jju-specific source. INPUT CONTRACT (when filled): Latin-script Jju orthography, already normalised. TONE: Jju is tonal and tone is not systematically written; unwritten tone would NOT be recoverable from the orthography. Wikidata supplies no ISO 639-3 or Glottolog code for this item (which is the Jju Wikipedia project), so those fields stay null rather than guessed; wikidata_qid likewise left unset.

sources.notes: Source of the phoneme inventory: 7 vowels, fortis-lenis (tense) contrast, plain/labialised/palatalised three-way consonant contrast, allophonic aspiration. Phonemic, not orthographic.
